Scenario.overview: Energy Complex The CostaRicto campaign targets energy organizations with a complex attack chain involving multiple stages, including initial access via spear-phishing, privilege escalation, lateral movement, and ultimately impacting energy production systems.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Financial gain and disruption of critical infrastructure

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Internal networks, Shared directories, End-user devices
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Phishing, Spear-phishing, Remote desktop, Web, Command and control

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The CostaRicto campaign begins by gathering information about target energy organizations using open-source intelligence techniques.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers develop custom tools using living-off-the-land binaries to evade detection and blend in with legitimate processes within the target network.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2024-37383 [CWE-807]: Vulnerability in email client allowing remote code execution
stage.description: The attackers gain initial access to the target network by sending spear-phishing emails with malicious attachments to high-value employees, exploiting a vulnerability in the email client.

Scenario.summary: The CostaRicto campaign employs a complex multi-stage attack strategy to gain unauthorized access to energy organizations, move laterally within their networks, and ultimately disrupt energy production systems.
